title: New migration and asylum rules enter into application: what is changing?
content: On 12 June, the Pact on Migration and Asylum entered into application, overhauling the EU's migration and asylum system. Now that the two-year transition period needed to implement the 10 legislative acts has ended, what changes can we expect?
snippet: On 12 June, the Pact on Migration and Asylum entered into application, overhauling the EU's migration and asylum system. Now that the two-year transition period needed to implement the 10 legislative

title: EU Coordinators discussed the strengthened European Child Guarantee and support for vulnerable young people
content: Representatives from EU countries gathered for the 25th meeting of the Child Guarantee Coordinators. Under the Cyprus Presidency, discussions focused on strengthening the European Child Guarantee and reviewing progress in its implementation.
snippet: Representatives from EU countries gathered for the 25th meeting of the Child Guarantee Coordinators. Under the Cyprus Presidency, discussions focused on strengthening the European Child Guarantee and

title: EU and Republic of Korea bolster strategic partnership with new areas of cooperation
content: The EU and the Republic of Korea have launched a High-Level Energy Dialogue to coordinate efforts on energy security, economic resilience, and the transition to clean energy.
snippet: The EU and the Republic of Korea have launched a High-Level Energy Dialogue to coordinate efforts on energy security, economic resilience, and the transition to clean energy.

title: Unlock Your Power: New EU campaign to help citizens better understand and use their energy rights
content: On 11 June the European Commission launched 'Unlock Your Power! Your energy. Your rights. Your choice.' - a new awareness-raising campaign designed to improve citizens’ understanding of their energy consumer rights.
snippet: On 11 June the European Commission launched 'Unlock Your Power! Your energy. Your rights. Your choice.' - a new awareness-raising campaign designed to improve citizens’ understanding of their energy

title: Protecting local food and drinks: More than 3,700 geographical indications registered
content: 3 700 names are now registered as geographical indications. Quality schemes protect the culinary heritage of Europe and add value to local food and drink products.
snippet: 3 700 names are now registered as geographical indications. Quality schemes protect the culinary heritage of Europe and add value to local food and drink products.

title: Municipal housing: Best practice leading the way across Europe
content: On 8 June, the European Commission hosted a dynamic workshop on “Developing an efficient ecosystem for municipal housing” in collaboration with Housing Europe, the federation of social, cooperative and public housing providers.
snippet: On 8 June, the European Commission hosted a dynamic workshop on “Developing an efficient ecosystem for municipal housing” in collaboration with Housing Europe, the federation of social, cooperative

title: Are you prepared for the safety risks of the modern workplace?
content: A safe and healthy working environment benefits all, whether you’re an employee or the boss. The latest episode of the Just Europe podcast tackles the hurdles involved and potential solutions.
snippet: A safe and healthy working environment benefits all, whether you’re an employee or the boss. The latest episode of the Just Europe podcast tackles the hurdles involved and potential solutions.

title: EU and Korea sign landmark digital trade agreement at summit
content: Today, the EU and the Republic of Korea signed a landmark Digital Trade Agreement (DTA) during the EU-Republic of Korea Summit held in Brussels.
snippet: Today, the EU and the Republic of Korea signed a landmark Digital Trade Agreement (DTA) during the EU-Republic of Korea Summit held in Brussels.

title: First-ever plans to unlock potential of EU islands and coastal communities
content: The European Commission has presented its first-ever, tailor-made strategies dedicated to support both EU islands and coastal communities and unlock their potential.
snippet: The European Commission has presented its first-ever, tailor-made strategies dedicated to support both EU islands and coastal communities and unlock their potential.

title: DIVERIS meeting calls for diversity and inclusion to become a core strategy in Europe’s space and defence sectors
content: The DIVERIS Annual Meeting 2026 has highlighted a clear shift in thinking across Europe’s space and defence sectors: diversity and inclusion can no longer be treated as a side effort or a box ticking exercise, but need to be part of core strategy, governance and investment decisions.
snippet: The DIVERIS Annual Meeting 2026 has highlighted a clear shift in thinking across Europe’s space and defence sectors: diversity and inclusion can no longer be treated as a side effort or a box ticking

title: EU and partners agree to strengthen Europe’s role in the orbital economy
content: The European Commission is advancing plans for a new pilot mission to perform tasks in orbit. Together with Greece, Italy, the Netherlands, Sweden, and Norway, the EU signed a joint declaration in support of the In-Space Operations and Services (ISOS) pilot mission, a step towards a European in-orbi
snippet: The European Commission is advancing plans for a new pilot mission to perform tasks in orbit. Together with Greece, Italy, the Netherlands, Sweden, and Norway, the EU signed a joint declaration in

title: Commission presents first-ever EU strategies for islands and coastal communities
content: Today, the European Commission adopted two dedicated strategies, one for EU islands and one for EU coastal communities, setting out for the first time a coordinated European approach to support both types of territories and unlock their long-term potential.
snippet: Today, the European Commission adopted two dedicated strategies, one for EU islands and one for EU coastal communities, setting out for the first time a coordinated European approach to support both

title: New Battery Booster set to inject €1.5 billion into the European battery industry
content: The EU is launching a new €1.5 billion Battery Booster Facility to help battery manufacturers scale up production in Europe. Backed by ETS revenues, the scheme will provide interest-free loans to accelerate investment, strengthen competitiveness and support Europe's clean-tech leadership.
snippet: The EU is launching a new €1.5 billion Battery Booster Facility to help battery manufacturers scale up production in Europe. Backed by ETS revenues, the scheme will provide interest-free loans to

title: Commission imposes interim measures on Meta to preserve free access to WhatsApp for rival AI assistants
content: The European Commission has ordered Meta to restore free access to WhatsApp for rival general-purpose AI assistants, and maintain it until the end of the Commission's antitrust investigation.
snippet: The European Commission has ordered Meta to restore free access to WhatsApp for rival general-purpose AI assistants, and maintain it until the end of the Commission's antitrust investigation.

title: Commission hosts 20th edition of the European Sustainable Energy Week
content: Taking place both onsite and online, this year's anniversary edition has the theme ‘A clean, secure and competitive Energy Union'.
snippet: Taking place both onsite and online, this year's anniversary edition has the theme ‘A clean, secure and competitive Energy Union'.

title: Two new members join the Alliance on Torture-Free Trade
content: On 9 June 2026, Andorra and Monaco joined the Alliance on Torture-Free Trade (Global Alliance).
snippet: On 9 June 2026, Andorra and Monaco joined the Alliance on Torture-Free Trade (Global Alliance).

title: EU to mobilise up to €25 billion for renewable energy and clean technology by 2035 through Pact for the Mediterranean initiative
content: Today, the European Union launches the Trans-Mediterranean Renewable Energy and Clean Tech Cooperation (T-MED) – a flagship initiative under the Pact for the Mediterranean.
snippet: Today, the European Union launches the Trans-Mediterranean Renewable Energy and Clean Tech Cooperation (T-MED) – a flagship initiative under the Pact for the Mediterranean.

title: Commission proposes €540 million and financial relief and other supports for farmers facing fertiliser crisis
content: Today, the European Commission presents measures to help farmers facing sharply rising fertiliser costs and to support Europe's food security, delivering on the recently announced Fertiliser Action Plan.
snippet: Today, the European Commission presents measures to help farmers facing sharply rising fertiliser costs and to support Europe's food security, delivering on the recently announced Fertiliser Action

title: Announcement of next auction (EU-Bills) on 17-06-2026
content: Auction announcement EU-Bills.
snippet: Auction announcement EU-Bills.

title: Commission welcomes Council agreement on strengthening CBAM
content: The European Commission welcomes the Council agreement to extend the Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) to specific downstream goods and reinforce existing anti-circumvention safeguards.
snippet: The European Commission welcomes the Council agreement to extend the Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) to specific downstream goods and reinforce existing anti-circumvention safeguards.

title: Commission approves €25 million Spanish State aid for fishing companies
content: The European Commission has approved a €25 million Spanish State aid scheme to support fishing companies facing increased fuel prices due to the Middle East crisis.
snippet: The European Commission has approved a €25 million Spanish State aid scheme to support fishing companies facing increased fuel prices due to the Middle East crisis.

title: Commission welcomes agreement on key safeguards for the new emissions trading system for buildings and road transport
content: The Commission welcomes a provisional agreement to strengthen safeguards for the new ETS2 carbon market covering buildings and road transport. The measures will enhance price stability, boost predictability for citizens and investors and support a smooth launch of the system in 2028.
snippet: The Commission welcomes a provisional agreement to strengthen safeguards for the new ETS2 carbon market covering buildings and road transport. The measures will enhance price stability, boost

title: CBAM webinar follow up: “How to make your decarbonisation efforts pay off”
content: On Thursday 7 May 2026, DG TAXUD hosted the second in a series of webinars on the implementation of the EU’s Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM).
snippet: On Thursday 7 May 2026, DG TAXUD hosted the second in a series of webinars on the implementation of the EU’s Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM).

title: New guidelines to improve EU sustainable urban mobility planning
content: The third edition (2026) of the Sustainable Urban Mobility Planning (SUMP) Guidelines will provide cities across Europe with improved support to plan, develop and implement sustainable urban mobility.
snippet: The third edition (2026) of the Sustainable Urban Mobility Planning (SUMP) Guidelines will provide cities across Europe with improved support to plan, develop and implement sustainable urban mobility.

title: Why a nature-positive economy is Europe's smartest investment
content: Read Commissioner Roswall's op-ed on restoring ecosystems following EU Green Week 2026.
snippet: Read Commissioner Roswall's op-ed on restoring ecosystems following EU Green Week 2026.

title: Commission steps up action on Europe's bioeconomy
content: Three complementary initiatives will be launched, including the Bio-based Europe Alliance to support scaling up the bioeconomy sector.
snippet: Three complementary initiatives will be launched, including the Bio-based Europe Alliance to support scaling up the bioeconomy sector.

title: New EU rules will improve cross-border rail traffic and optimise network capacity
content: The European Union has adopted new rules on the use of railway infrastructure capacity, marking a significant step towards a more efficient, coordinated and reliable European rail network.
snippet: The European Union has adopted new rules on the use of railway infrastructure capacity, marking a significant step towards a more efficient, coordinated and reliable European rail network.

title: EU concludes modernised Economic Partnership Agreement with Sub-Saharan Africa partners
content: Today, the EU and four Eastern and Southern Africa (ESA) States (Comoros, Madagascar, Mauritius, and Seychelles) concluded negotiations on an enhanced Economic Partnership Agreement (EPA).
snippet: Today, the EU and four Eastern and Southern Africa (ESA) States (Comoros, Madagascar, Mauritius, and Seychelles) concluded negotiations on an enhanced Economic Partnership Agreement (EPA).

title: Eurostat: Energy use in EU households drops for 3 years in a row
content: In 2024, EU households used 9.54 million terajoules of energy, a slight decline (-0.2%) from 9.57 million terajoules in 2023.
snippet: In 2024, EU households used 9.54 million terajoules of energy, a slight decline (-0.2%) from 9.57 million terajoules in 2023.

title: PES Network publishes 15 new PES practices
content: The PES Network has added 15 new practices to its PES Practices database, showcasing how Public Employment Services across Europe are responding to labour market changes and evolving skills needs.
snippet: The PES Network has added 15 new practices to its PES Practices database, showcasing how Public Employment Services across Europe are responding to labour market changes and evolving skills needs.

title: Travelling abroad in the summer? Don’t forget your European Health Insurance Card
content: Travelling abroad for holidays is exciting, but accidents can happen. While you are travelling, the European Health Insurance Card gives you access to necessary medical services in Europe.
snippet: Travelling abroad for holidays is exciting, but accidents can happen. While you are travelling, the European Health Insurance Card gives you access to necessary medical services in Europe.

title: Eurostat: EU sold €10.4 billion worth of wind turbines in 2024
content: Between 2014 and 2015, the sold production quantity of wind-powered generating sets in the EU fell from 22 200 to 8 300. From 2015 to 2023, the number varied each year between 6 800 and 12 000, before increasing to 15 500 in 2024.
